title: Assignments should not be redundant
description: Assignments should not be redundantAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellredundantWhy is this an issue?The transitive property says that ifa == bandb == c, thena == c. In such cases, there’s no point in assigningatocor vice versa because they’re already equivalent.This rule raises an issue when an assignment is useless because the assigned-to variable already holds the value on all execution paths.Noncompliant code examplea = b; c = a; b = c; // Noncompliant: c and b are already the sameCompliant solutiona = b; c = a;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Avoid redundant assignments and suspicious comparisons.
